mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals a broad demand collapse that forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline concurrently, with a hit rate below 40 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates expanding corporate capacity and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises during post-shock recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings compress real equity valuations through inflation risk premia, generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no negative correlation with CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption expenditures growth exceeding 5 percent year-over-year signals robust consumer spending that supports corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of months where RSAFS YoY growth surpasses 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and stagnant unemployment rates indicates labor market tightness that forces wage inflation and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power with a coefficient p-value above 0.10 in a quarterly regression model over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases above 0.5 percent during a monetary tightening cycle compress equity multiples through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5 percent during tightening phases.
